text.title: Vector Databases — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules: pick DB by operational profile, pin embedding model with metric, mandatory tenant filter, hybrid search as default, metric matches embedding training objective.

rule.statement: DB MUST be picked using the decision tree (Postgres-exists, scale tier, managed vs self-host, hybrid need). Picking by "popularity" or "what we already know" is forbidden in production.

rationale.source: 2026 vector DB benchmarks; production deployment lessons

rule.rationale: Qdrant suits filtered self-host; pgvector wins when Postgres exists; Pinecone removes ops burden; Milvus at billion-scale. Wrong pick costs migration later.

rule.statement: Collection MUST pin embedding model name + version AND distance metric. Re-indexing is required on model change. Mixing embeddings from different models in one collection is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Different models live in different vector spaces; mixing produces nonsense neighbours. Pinning makes re-index a calendar event, not a silent drift.

rule.statement: Multi-tenant collections MUST enforce tenant_id filter at the query layer (function signature requires it). Missing filter returns cross-tenant data silently.
rule.rationale: Vector queries without tenant filter succeed and return results from all tenants. The leak is invisible in logs because query "succeeded". Make tenant_id a required parameter at the function-signature level.

rule.statement: Production search MUST use hybrid (vector + BM25 fused via RRF). Pure vector search is forbidden as the only retrieval mode for production search-style queries.

rationale.source: industry benchmarks; Pinecone / Qdrant hybrid docs

rule.rationale: Pure vector search misses exact-match (product codes, names, technical terms). Hybrid lifts recall@10 15-25%. Both Qdrant and Pinecone support natively.

rule.statement: Distance metric MUST match the embedding model's training objective: cosine for L2-normalised dense embeddings (OpenAI text-embedding-3, Cohere embed), dot product for non-normalised, euclidean only when training docs say so.

rationale.source: embedding model docs; metric mismatch post-mortems

rule.rationale: Using euclidean on OpenAI embeddings (cosine-trained) produces silently wrong rankings. The model just returns "results" without flagging metric mismatch.
